MSI - Outreach

Welcome to the Molecular Sciences Institute Outreach Website. We hope that you will find this website useful, whether you are a middle school student interested in learning about science fairs, a high school student beginning to think about a career, or a college student or PhD considering research options in the field of genomics. Our program provides opportunities and training for almost everyone, and this website points you to opportunities which might be of interest to you.

***** L&S126 at UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA BERKELEY *****. Dr. Roger Brent co-teaches an important and timely course at the University of California, Berkeley, with Dr. Paul Rabinow, geared toward understanding the science and societal impacts of genomics.

The course, "Towards an Anthropology of Biology: Genomics and Citizenship" (LNS 126), is part of a series of prestigious multidisciplinary offerings from the UC Berkeley College of Letters and Sciences, which fosters the ideals of a liberal arts education at the highest levels of excellence. The course will provide an overview of the most pressing issues in genomic biology, the world in which genomic biology functions, and the world that it has shaped.

Students, please see important information at COURSE READING.

What is Genomics?

Genomics is the study of the full genetic map of life that is specific to every species and, due to genetic variation, unique to every organism. Genomics utilizes a variety of techniques for obtaining actual genomic data in the form of nucleotide sequences (sequencing), as well as studying the effects and functions of discrete portions of the genome at the gene level.

Why Study Genomics?

Genomics is an example of predictive biology. This means that by understanding the genome(s) of a species or species', we can understand the causes of its behavior, interaction with its environment and eventually predict the effect on that species of various stimuli. Even at the cellular level we may at some point be able to predict the effects of a drug or an environmental factor that may allow us to create cures for diseases and to understand what's causing them.

If at any time you wish to return to this page, the "Outreach" link on the navigation bar at left will always return you here.

With questions or comments about any of the MSI Outreach programs, please contact Maryanne McCormick.
